items.title: This $28 million ether market bet aims to profit from pure market chaos
items.description: The mega trade aims to profit from a potential surge in ether price turbulence.

items.title: Bitcoin’s anti-spam fight gets a 'DOG Mode' reply
items.description: While BIP 110 wants to restrict data through a consensus change and has almost no miner support, a new DOG Mode client wants the opposite and requires no vote at all.

items.title: Bitcoin under $63,000 after new U.S. strike on Iran. Trump's China comment adds to uncertainty
items.description: Geopolitical tensions and renewed fears of U.S.-China frictions are weighing on risk assets, including bitcoin.

items.title: $1.9 trillion asset manager T. Rowe Price bets on active management with first multi-token crypto ETF
items.description: T. Rowe Price launched what it says is the industry's first actively managed multi-token spot crypto ETF, offering diversified exposure to digital assets.

items.title: Citadel Securities invests $400 million in Crypto.com, valuing exchange at $20 billion
items.description: The exchange's first institutional funding round values it at $20 billion and will fund expansion into tokenized securities and derivatives.

items.title: Stripe’s $53 billion PayPal bid is a high-stakes play to own the future of digital payments
items.description: If Stripe acquires PayPal, the real prize could be consumer wallets, stablecoin issuance or the infrastructure powering the next generation of digital payments.

items.title: Visa backs Open USD with new stablecoin platform as Circle faces fresh competition
items.description: Visa launched a stablecoin platform that lets banks and fintechs issue, manage and settle digital dollars through its payments network.

items.title: Keyrock acquires BlockFills trading assets to expand institutional crypto business
items.description: The deal adds institutional trading clients, derivatives expertise and regulatory licenses as Keyrock looks to deepen its presence in crypto capital markets.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs add $368M in three-day buying streak
items.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs attracted $79.2 million on Thursday, lifting their three-day inflow total to about $368 million as Bitcoin attempted a price recovery.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Ordinals advocate Leonidas proposes new Bitcoin client: ‘$DOG Mode’
items.description: “Over time the economic incentives will drive $DOG Mode’s adoption and force Bitcoin Core to stop gatekeeping and allow these completely valid transactions,” said Leonidas.

items.title: Crypto exchange Bybit launches in Indonesia after NOBI acquisition
items.description: The launch gives Bybit a locally operated platform in one of Asia’s largest crypto markets, home to more than 21 million crypto exchange users.

items.title: Trump Media sells Wall Street low-latency access to Trump posts
items.description: The paid Truth API is aimed at high-frequency trading firms seeking “the fastest” access to market-moving Truth Social posts, including those from US President Donald Trump.

items.title: Balaji seeks Malaysia deal, threatens exit after Network School probe
items.description: “If we are not welcome, there are many other countries that would welcome us,” said Network School founder Balaji Srinivasan in a message to the Malaysian prime minister.

items.title: Bitcoin liquidity clusters determine BTC’s price direction as futures flow fuels price
items.description: Bitcoin futures traders are propping up the current market move, while liquidation heatmap data provides hints on where BTC price may go next.

items.title: Citadel Securities invests $400M in Crypto.com at $20B valuation
items.description: Crypto exchanges have increasingly positioned themselves as bridges between digital asset markets and traditional finance.

items.title: Trump doubles down on 2020 election claims in national address, alleging China meddling
items.description: Trump also repeated his calls for the Republican-led Congress to pass the "SAVE America Act," a controversial election bill that lacks support to pass.

items.title: Xi pitches China as AI partner to developing world, warns against risks and security overreach
items.description: China will provide developing countries with 5,000 opportunities in AI training and seminar programs, as well as develop AI cooperation with various blocs.

items.title: World’s largest olive oil producer gives upbeat outlook after historic industry crisis
items.description: Spain's Deoleo, the world's largest olive oil producer, says the industry is gradually recovering from one of the most challenging moments in its history.

items.title: Oil prices rise as U.S.-Iran hostilities threaten supply through Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Oil prices rose Friday as investors weighed escalating threats between the U.S. and Iran.

items.title: Sweden's Saab posts record orders as fighter jet maker cashes in on higher defense spending
items.description: European governments ramp up defense spending and book orders with the region's companies.

items.title: Netflix stock falls as earnings forecast disappoints, company says it will give fewer engagement updates
items.description: The streaming giant said it would cut back on the frequency of its "What We Watched" reports, which provide a picture of engagement.

items.title: India's biggest IPO this year rakes in bids worth $31 billion, powered by institutional frenzy
items.description: The IPO of India's largest asset manager closed on Thursday, drawing keen interest from institutional investors.

items.title: Elon Musk's Memphis AI empire is the epicenter of the data center backlash
items.description: Data center-related policy proposals, protests and litigation are underway across the country citing Colossus and Memphis as a cautionary tale.

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DfHQJwqiOzc! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Micron has turned into ‘the most important stock in the market.’ So is it time to worry?
items.description: While investors are concerned about a memory peak, analysts say the stock still looks cheap relative to various earnings scenarios.

items.title: Netflix is getting stingier about its viewing data, and Wall Street isn’t happy
items.description: Netflix’s stock is falling in the wake of mixed earnings and a new plan to cut back on the publication of ‘What We Watched’ reports.

items.title: 10 U.S. metro areas where builders are slashing prices on new homes
items.description: Existing-home prices just hit a new record high — but there are plenty of newly built homes selling at a discount, as desperate builders cut prices.

items.title: Palo Alto Networks’ stock has been on a tear — and it could go even higher, according to these bulls
items.description: The company’s shares have roughly doubled over the past three months, as investors have warmed to the idea that AI is increasing the need for cybersecurity products.

items.title: As SpaceX’s stock notches first close below IPO price, the company is gearing up for a crucial launch
items.description: The megarocket’s success is SpaceX’s “single most important watch item,” one analyst says.

items.title: Americans say they need $1.2 million to retire. Most are too deep in debt to get there.
items.description: More than 80% of Americans worry about running out of money in retirement: “It really keeps them up at night.”

items.title: GE boosts profit outlook, but stock falls as booming order growth cools
items.description: GE Aerospace’s stock falls after earnings, again, with recently rapid order-book growth slowing.

items.title: ‘It’s heartbreaking’: My brother claimed Social Security at 70. He died from cancer after one payment. Why wait to claim?
items.description: “I’ve always been a little skeptical of the government’s encouragement to delay claiming benefits.”

Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pdXNYBSIvhU! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Truth Social Parent to Sell Faster Access to Trump’s Posts
items.description: The product, Truth API, could give Wall Street trading firms an edge in seeing President Trump’s posts on the social media platform he partly owns.

items.title: Netflix Revenue Grows 13% to $12.6 Billion
items.description: The company’s second-quarter earnings were largely in line with Wall Street’s expectations.

items.title: Mary-Dell Chilton Dies at 87; Helped Create First Genetically Modified Plant
items.description: In 1982, she led the research team that figured out how to genetically alter plants, a discovery that would eventually transform global agriculture.

items.title: White House Faces Stiff Pushback on Subjecting Grants to Political Review
items.description: Academics, city leaders and congressional lawmakers number among the thousands to urge the Trump administration to reconsider a plan to assert more control over grants.

items.title: Americans Are Spending, and Not Just on Necessities
items.description: Lower gas prices have been a relief for households struggling with higher living costs. But now they are creeping higher again.

items.title: Maker of Drone Boats Used in Iran War Will Open New Texas Shipyard
items.description: Saronic Technologies will make naval and commercial vessels at the facility. The company’s naval drone helped rescue the crew of a downed Apache helicopter last month.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz Tanker Traffic Erodes Further as Oil Prices Rise
items.description: Very few ships passed through the waterway on the first full day of the U.S. naval blockade of Iran.
